description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a green upper and lower wing, each featuring roundel insignias, and a blue fuselage. The aircraft has a classic open cockpit situated near the front, with a visible propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section is supported by a lightweight truss structure, leading to a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with matching roundel markings. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft, with exposed framework and fabric-covered wings.
description: A vintage green biplane with roundel insignias, blue fuselage, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear. Features exposed truss tail and dual wings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual green wings, blue nose with propeller, and open cockpit behind the propeller.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the green wings with roundels, blue fuselage, and exposed truss tail structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with roundel on the horizontal stabilizer, truss framework, and both wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the green wings, blue cockpit, and the tail's structural details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; moderate dihedral angle; no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the propeller.
